content_en:
The people of knowledge have mentioned some of the wisdoms behind the prohibition of drinking directly from the mouth of a jug, and we will mention a few of them here. First, the more people drink from and breathe into a jug, canteen, or bottle, the worse will become its smell, until it becomes so bad that one will feel aversion at the idea of drinking from it. Second, a large ; canteen or jug might have filth, dirt, or insects inside of it, anda person who drinks from it won't be able to notice those things, : and as a result, his health might become affected. But if he pours - the drink into a glass, he will be able to see the filth and remove .

"1! Related by Bukhaaree (5627), Ahmad (7113), and others. "1 Related by Bukhaaree (5629), Ahmad (1990), At-Tirmidhee (1825), An- -

Nassaaee (4448), Abu Daawood (3719), Ibn Maajah (3421), and Ad-

Chapter - 9 The Manners Of Eating And Drinking 223

it. Third, a person’s spit might get mixed into the drink, and others will feel disgusted as a result. Fourth, the spit and breath of the person drinking might make someone else fall ill, for it is a medically established fact that contagious diseases sometimes spread through saliva and breath.

Question: It is established in an authentic hadith that the Prophet # drank from a hanging canteen. How then can we find harmony between his action -~ which indicates permissibility - and his verbal prohibition?

Answer: Refuting the claim that the action abrogates the verbal prohibition in this issue, Ibn Hajr said, “In Sharhut-Tirmidhee, our Shaikh said, ‘We must make a distinction between situations wherein one has no excuse - so that the prohibition stands - and situations wherein one has an excuse, such as when a canteen is hanging up, and the person who wants to drink cannot easily find a cup and is not positioned such that he can drink from his hand. When one has an excuse, it is not disliked for him to drink from the mouth of a canteen, as is the case in the aforementioned Ahadeeth.’ This view [of our Shaikh] is corroborated by the fact that, in all of the Ahadeeth that indicate permissibility, the canteen was hanging up. [We must keep in mind that] drinking from a hanging container is more specific (thus allowing it to take a different ruling) than drinking from a container in general. Consequently, nothing in those Ahadeeth points to a general, comprehensive ruling of permissibility; instead, permissibility applies to that one situation only. [In finding harmony between the apparently conflicting narrations] it is better to construe some of the narrations as dealing with cases of necessity, thus bringing complete harmony between the different narrations, than to take the view of abrogation. And Allah 3 knows best.’

" The hadith is related by At-Tirmidhee (1892) with the fallowing wording: Kabshah Al-Ansaariyyah 4 said, “The Messenger of Allah #% entered upon me and then drank, standing up, from the mouth of a hanging canteen...” Abu ‘Eesa At-Tirmidhee said, “This hadith is Hasan Sahech.” Tbn Maajah related it too (3423), and Al-Albaanee gave it a ‘sahih’ grade.

18) The Person Who Is Serving Drinks To Others Should Be The Last Of Them To Drink Himself

The basis of this ruling is a long hadith related by Qataadah #, in which he 4 said, ‘The Messenger of Allah #% began to pour and I to hand them the drink, until no one remained save me and save the Messenger of Allah #%. Then the Messenger of Allah # poured [again] and said [to me], ‘Drink,’ to which I answered, ‘I will not drink until you drink, O Messenger of Allah.’ He # said,

Gy Biel pili gale Sp

“The person who gives drink to the people is (i.e., should be) the last of them to drink.’

So I drank and [then] the Messenger of Allah 3% drank...”")

19) It ls Recommended To Talk During A Meal

In Thyaa Uloom Ad-Deen, Al-Ghazaalee pointed out that we should talk during a meal so as to differ from people of foreign nations, for it customary for them to remain silent during meals.) And we have been ordered not to imitate non-Muslims. Tbn Muflaih related that Ishaaq Ibn Ibraaheem said, “I once had dinner with Abu ‘Abdur-Rahmaan (Ahmad Ibn Hanbal) and a relative of his. When we stopped talking, he ate and continued to say, ‘Alhamdulillah (All praise is to Allah)’ and ‘Bismillah (In the Name of Allah).’ He then said, ‘Eating and praising [Allah] is better than eating and remaining silent.” After relating this account, Ibn Muflaih said, “I found nothing related from Ahmad that explicitly conflicted with this narration, nor did I find any opposition from most of Al-Ashaab (scholars that followed Imam Ahmad’s principles of jurisprudence). It appears that Ahmad - may Allah have mercy on him - followed some narration in this

'] Related by Muslim (681), Ahmad (22040), At-Tirmidhee (1894), Ibn Maajah (3434), and Ad-Daarimee (2135). Some of them related it in its full length, while others only related the portion that contains proof for the issue in question. And yet others related it both ways, in its full length and in truncated form.

1 Refer to Thyaa ‘Uloom Ad-Deen (2/11)

Chapter - 9 The Manners Of Eating And Drinking 225

regard, for it was his way and wont to search out for ways to follow [the Prophet #].””

20) Eating Together As A Group

It is recommended for people to eat together as a group, for doing so causes their food to be blessed: the more the people are that are sharing a meal, the more the blessings. Jaabir Ibn ‘Abdullah 4 reported that he heard the Messenger of Allah # say,

“The food of one is enough for two; the food of two is enough for four; and the food of four is enough for eight."

The link between a meal being blessed and people sharing a meal is more explicitly worded in this narration, which At-Tabaraanee relates from the hadith of Ibn ‘Umar : “Eat together and do not part from one another, for indeed, the food of one is sufficient for two...” We learn from this hadith that it is the blessings of gathering and sharing that lead to a seemingly small amount of food being sufficient for many; hence, the more the people there are that are sharing, the more blessings will there be in their meal.

Wahshee Ibn Harb related from his father, who related from his father, that the Companions of the Messenger of Allah 2 said, “O Messenger of Allah, we eat but do not become filled.” He # said,

“Perhaps you separate from one another [when you eat].” They said, “Yes.” He 2% said,

1 Related by Muslim (2059), Ahmad (13810), At-Tirmidhee (1820), Ibn Maajah (3254), and Ad-Daarimee (2044).

“Then gather upon your food (i.e., eat together) and mention

Allah’s name over it, and as a result, it will become blessed for “Al

21) It Is Disliked To Eat A Lot Of Food Or To Eat So Little That One's Body Weakens

Eating too much leads to many diseases, causes laziness, making one feel it heavy and burdensome to do deeds of obedience to Allah 4é, and hardens a person's heart - and we seek refuge in Allah #€ from all of that. The opposite way, eating too little, also weakens a person’s body, making him too weak to do acts of obedience to Allah 4%. The best cure and prevention is what is prescribed by the Prophet #%, and were we to follow the Prophet’s way, we would not, in many instances, have to pay a visit to the doctor. Miqdaam Ibn Ma’dee Karib 4 reported that he heard the Messenger of Allah # say,

“A human being has never filled a container that is worse than his stomach, It is sufficient for the son of Adam to have just that amount of morsels that cause his back to remain upright. But if there is no escape (i.e., he wants more), then one third for his food, one third for his drink, and one third for his breathing.’!

We would do we" to heed what some of our pious predecessors had to say about this subject. Ibn ‘Abdul-Barr and others related that, one day, when ‘Umar Ibn Al-Khattaab # delivered a sermon, he said, ‘‘Beware of gluttony, for it makes one lazy from prayer and it is harmful to the body. Upon you is moderation in your food, for that will keep you further away

"] Related by Abu Daawood (3764), and Al-Albaanee gave a ‘Sakeeh’ grade to his narration; Ahmad (15648); and Ibn Maajah (3286).

"1 Related by At-Tirmidhee (2380), who said, “Hasan sahih”; by Tbn Maajah (3349), and Al-Albaanee gave a ‘sahih’ grade to his narration; and by

Chapter - 9 The Manners Of Eating And Drinking 207 from ungratefulness, healthier in your body, and stronger to do worship. And a person does not become destroyed until he prefers his desire over his religion.” ‘Alee 2 said, ‘The stomach is the tank of the body: liquids come into it and flow out of it. If it is healthy, liquids will flow out of it in a healthy manner, but if it is sick, liquids will flow out of it with sickness.” Fudail Ibn ‘Iyaadh said, “Two actions harden the heart: much talk and much eating.” And it was said to Imam Ahmad, “These are the people who eat very little food.” He said, “That does not impress me. I heard ‘Abdur-Rahmaan Ibn Mahdee say, ‘A group of people did the same and it resulted in them being cut off from obligatory duties (ie., their bodies became so weak that they became negligent in performing obligatory duties).”

22) The Prohibition Of Sitting At A Table Upon Which Alcohol Is Imbibed

‘Umar Ibn Al-Khattaab # said, ‘The Messenger of Allah 2% forbade two kinds of eating: sitting at a table upon which alcohol is imbibed, and for a man to eat while he is lying prone on his stomach." Imam Ahmad related the same with this wording: “Whosoever believes in Allah and the Last Day, then let him not sit at a table upon which alcohol is imbibed...” Even when one doesn’t drink alcohol, just by sitting with others who are drinking it, he shows his tacit acceptance and approval of their evil action. And that is the wisdom behind the prohibition.

1) Related by Abu Daawood (3774), and Al-Albaanee gave a ‘sahih’ grade to his narration; and by Ibn Maajah (3370), but without mention of the first part of the hadith.

The Manners Of Going To The Bathroom

Salmaan # said, “[One of] the disbelievers said to us, ‘Verily, we see that your companion (the Prophet #%) even teaches you how to relieve yourselves.”” Salmaan # answered him, “Yes, he forbids each of us from cleaning himself [after urinating or defecating] with his right hand and from facing the Qiblah [during the act of urinating or defecating]. And he prohibited the use of manure or bones [for cleaning]. And he #¢ said,
content_id:
Para ulama telah menyebutkan beberapa hikmah di balik larangan minum langsung dari mulut kendi, dan kami akan menyebutkan beberapa di antaranya di sini. Pertama, semakin banyak orang yang minum dari dan menghembuskan napas ke dalam kendi, tempat minum, atau botol, semakin buruk baunya, hingga menjadi begitu tidak sedap sehingga seseorang akan merasa enggan untuk minum darinya. Kedua, kendi atau tempat minum yang besar mungkin mengandung kotoran, debu, atau serangga di dalamnya, dan orang yang minum darinya tidak akan dapat memperhatikan hal-hal tersebut, sehingga kesehatannya bisa terganggu. Namun jika ia menuangkan minuman ke dalam gelas, ia akan dapat melihat kotoran tersebut dan membuangnya.

"1! Diriwayatkan oleh Bukhaaree (5627), Ahmad (7113), dan lainnya. "1 Diriwayatkan oleh Bukhaaree (5629), Ahmad (1990), At-Tirmidhee (1825), An-

Nassaaee (4448), Abu Daawood (3719), Ibn Maajah (3421), dan Ad-

Bab - 9 Adab Makan dan Minum 223

it. Ketiga, ludah seseorang mungkin bercampur ke dalam minuman, dan orang lain akan merasa jijik karenanya. Keempat, ludah dan napas orang yang minum mungkin dapat menularkan penyakit kepada orang lain, karena telah terbukti secara medis bahwa penyakit menular kadang menyebar melalui air liur dan napas.

Pertanyaan: Telah ditetapkan dalam hadith yang shahih bahwa Nabi # minum dari tempat minum yang digantung. Bagaimana kita dapat mengharmoniskan perbuatan beliau — yang menunjukkan kebolehan — dengan larangan verbalnya?

Jawaban: Menolak klaim bahwa perbuatan tersebut menghapus larangan verbal dalam masalah ini, Ibn Hajr berkata, "Dalam Sharhut-Tirmidhee, Syaikh kami berkata, 'Kita harus membedakan antara situasi di mana seseorang tidak memiliki uzur — sehingga larangan tetap berlaku — dan situasi di mana seseorang memiliki uzur, seperti ketika tempat minum digantung, dan orang yang ingin minum tidak dapat dengan mudah menemukan gelas dan tidak berada dalam posisi yang memungkinkan ia minum dengan tangannya. Apabila seseorang memiliki uzur, maka tidak dimakruhkan baginya untuk minum dari mulut tempat minum, sebagaimana yang disebutkan dalam hadith-hadith tersebut.' Pandangan [Syaikh kami] ini didukung oleh kenyataan bahwa, dalam semua hadith yang menunjukkan kebolehan, tempat minum tersebut dalam keadaan digantung. [Kita harus mengingat bahwa] minum dari wadah yang digantung lebih khusus (sehingga memungkinkan penerapan hukum yang berbeda) daripada minum dari wadah secara umum. Akibatnya, tidak ada satu pun dari hadith-hadith tersebut yang menunjukkan hukum kebolehan yang bersifat umum dan menyeluruh; sebaliknya, kebolehan hanya berlaku untuk satu situasi tersebut saja. [Dalam menemukan keharmonisan antara riwayat-riwayat yang tampaknya bertentangan] lebih baik menafsirkan sebagian riwayat sebagai berkaitan dengan kondisi darurat, sehingga membawa keharmonisan yang sempurna di antara berbagai riwayat, daripada mengambil pandangan nasakh. Dan Allah 3 Maha Mengetahui.'

" Hadith ini diriwayatkan oleh At-Tirmidhee (1892) dengan redaksi berikut: Kabshah Al-Ansaariyyah 4 berkata, "Rasulullah #% masuk menemuiku lalu minum, dalam keadaan berdiri, dari mulut tempat minum yang digantung..." Abu 'Eesa At-Tirmidhee berkata, "Hadith ini Hasan Sahih." Ibn Maajah juga meriwayatkannya (3423), dan Al-Albaanee memberikan penilaian 'shahih'.

18) Orang Yang Menuangkan Minuman Kepada Orang Lain Hendaknya Menjadi Yang Terakhir Minum

Dasar hukum ini adalah sebuah hadith panjang yang diriwayatkan oleh Qataadah #, di mana beliau berkata, 'Rasulullah #% mulai menuangkan dan aku menyerahkan minuman kepada mereka, hingga tidak ada seorang pun yang tersisa kecuali aku dan Rasulullah #%. Kemudian Rasulullah #% menuangkan [lagi] dan berkata [kepadaku], 'Minumlah,' dan aku menjawab, 'Aku tidak akan minum sampai engkau minum, wahai Rasulullah.' Beliau # berkata,

Gy Biel pili gale Sp

"Orang yang memberikan minuman kepada orang-orang adalah (yakni, hendaknya) yang terakhir di antara mereka untuk minum.'

Maka aku pun minum dan [kemudian] Rasulullah 3% minum...'

19) Dianjurkan Untuk Berbicara Saat Makan

Dalam Ihya Uloom Ad-Deen, Al-Ghazaalee menunjukkan bahwa kita seharusnya berbicara saat makan agar berbeda dari orang-orang bangsa asing, karena sudah menjadi kebiasaan mereka untuk diam selama makan. Dan kita telah diperintahkan untuk tidak meniru orang-orang non-Muslim. Ibn Muflaih meriwayatkan bahwa Ishaaq Ibn Ibraaheem berkata, "Suatu ketika aku makan malam bersama Abu 'Abdur-Rahmaan (Ahmad Ibn Hanbal) dan salah seorang kerabatnya. Ketika kami berhenti berbicara, beliau makan dan terus mengucapkan, 'Alhamdulillah (Segala puji bagi Allah)' dan 'Bismillah (Dengan nama Allah).' Kemudian beliau berkata, 'Makan sambil memuji [Allah] lebih baik daripada makan sambil diam.'" Setelah meriwayatkan kisah ini, Ibn Muflaih berkata, "Aku tidak menemukan sesuatu pun yang diriwayatkan dari Ahmad yang secara eksplisit bertentangan dengan riwayat ini, dan aku juga tidak menemukan penentangan dari sebagian besar Al-Ashaab (para ulama yang mengikuti prinsip-prinsip fikih Imam Ahmad). Tampaknya Ahmad — semoga Allah merahmatinya — mengikuti suatu riwayat dalam hal ini

'] Diriwayatkan oleh Muslim (681), Ahmad (22040), At-Tirmidhee (1894), Ibn Maajah (3434), dan Ad-Daarimee (2135). Sebagian dari mereka meriwayatkannya secara lengkap, sementara yang lain hanya meriwayatkan bagian yang mengandung dalil untuk masalah yang dibahas. Dan yang lainnya meriwayatkannya dengan kedua cara, secara lengkap maupun dalam bentuk ringkas.

1 Merujuk kepada Ihya 'Uloom Ad-Deen (2/11)

Bab - 9 Adab Makan dan Minum 225

karena sudah menjadi kebiasaan dan watak beliau untuk mencari cara-cara mengikuti [Nabi #]."

20) Makan Bersama Secara Berkelompok

Dianjurkan bagi orang-orang untuk makan bersama secara berkelompok, karena hal itu menyebabkan makanan mereka menjadi berkah: semakin banyak orang yang berbagi makanan, semakin banyak berkahnya. Jaabir Ibn 'Abdullah 4 melaporkan bahwa ia mendengar Rasulullah # bersabda,

"Makanan satu orang cukup untuk dua orang; makanan dua orang cukup untuk empat orang; dan makanan empat orang cukup untuk delapan orang."

Kaitan antara makanan yang berkah dengan orang-orang yang berbagi makanan diungkapkan lebih eksplisit dalam riwayat ini, yang diriwayatkan oleh At-Tabaraanee dari hadith Ibn 'Umar: "Makanlah bersama dan janganlah berpisah satu sama lain, karena sesungguhnya makanan satu orang cukup untuk dua orang..." Kita pelajari dari hadith ini bahwa keberkahan berkumpul dan berbagi itulah yang menyebabkan makanan yang tampaknya sedikit menjadi cukup untuk banyak orang; oleh karena itu, semakin banyak orang yang berbagi, semakin banyak pula keberkahan dalam makanan mereka.

Wahshee Ibn Harb meriwayatkan dari ayahnya, yang meriwayatkan dari kakeknya, bahwa para Sahabat Rasulullah 2 berkata, "Wahai Rasulullah, kami makan namun tidak merasa kenyang." Beliau # bersabda,

"Barangkali kalian makan secara terpisah satu sama lain." Mereka berkata, "Ya." Beliau 2% bersabda,

1 Diriwayatkan oleh Muslim (2059), Ahmad (13810), At-Tirmidhee (1820), Ibn Maajah (3254), dan Ad-Daarimee (2044).

"Maka berkumpullah atas makananmu (yakni, makanlah bersama) dan sebutlah nama Allah atasnya, maka makanan itu akan menjadi berkah bagimu."

21) Dimakruhkan Makan Terlalu Banyak atau Makan Terlalu Sedikit Hingga Tubuh Menjadi Lemah

Makan terlalu banyak menimbulkan banyak penyakit, menyebabkan kemalasan, membuat seseorang merasa berat dan terbebani dalam melakukan amal ketaatan kepada Allah 4é, dan mengeraskan hati seseorang — dan kita berlindung kepada Allah #€ dari semua itu. Sebaliknya, makan terlalu sedikit juga melemahkan tubuh seseorang, membuatnya terlalu lemah untuk melakukan amal ketaatan kepada Allah 4%. Penyembuhan dan pencegahan terbaik adalah apa yang telah disyariatkan oleh Nabi #%, dan seandainya kita mengikuti cara Nabi, dalam banyak keadaan kita tidak perlu mengunjungi dokter. Miqdaam Ibn Ma'dee Karib 4 melaporkan bahwa ia mendengar Rasulullah # bersabda,

"Manusia tidak pernah memenuhi wadah yang lebih buruk dari perutnya sendiri. Cukuplah bagi anak Adam beberapa suapan yang dapat menegakkan punggungnya. Namun jika tidak bisa dihindari (yakni, ia ingin lebih), maka sepertiga untuk makanannya, sepertiga untuk minumannya, dan sepertiga untuk napasnya."

Kita akan sangat beruntung jika memperhatikan apa yang dikatakan oleh sebagian pendahulu kita yang saleh mengenai masalah ini. Ibn 'Abdul-Barr dan lainnya meriwayatkan bahwa, suatu hari, ketika 'Umar Ibn Al-Khattaab # menyampaikan khutbah, beliau berkata, "Jauhilah sifat rakus dalam makan, karena hal itu membuat seseorang malas shalah dan berbahaya bagi tubuh. Hendaklah kalian bersikap moderat dalam makan, karena hal itu akan menjauhkan kalian

"] Diriwayatkan oleh Abu Daawood (3764), dan Al-Albaanee memberikan penilaian 'shahih' terhadap riwayatnya; Ahmad (15648); dan Ibn Maajah (3286).

"1 Diriwayatkan oleh At-Tirmidhee (2380), yang berkata, "Hasan shahih"; oleh Ibn Maajah (3349), dan Al-Albaanee memberikan penilaian 'shahih' terhadap riwayatnya; dan oleh

Bab - 9 Adab Makan dan Minum 207 dari sifat kufur nikmat, lebih menyehatkan tubuhmu, dan lebih menguatkanmu untuk beribadah. Dan seseorang tidak akan celaka kecuali jika ia mendahulukan hawa nafsunya atas agamanya." 'Alee 2 berkata, "Perut adalah wadah tubuh: cairan masuk dan keluar darinya. Jika ia sehat, cairan akan mengalir keluar darinya dengan sehat, namun jika ia sakit, cairan akan mengalir keluar darinya dengan membawa penyakit." Fudail Ibn 'Iyaadh berkata, "Dua perbuatan yang mengeraskan hati: banyak bicara dan banyak makan." Dan dikatakan kepada Imam Ahmad, "Inilah orang-orang yang makan sangat sedikit." Beliau berkata, "Hal itu tidak membuatku kagum. Aku mendengar 'Abdur-Rahmaan Ibn Mahdee berkata, 'Sekelompok orang melakukan hal yang sama dan akibatnya mereka terputus dari kewajiban-kewajiban (yakni, tubuh mereka menjadi begitu lemah sehingga mereka lalai dalam melaksanakan kewajiban-kewajiban).'"

22) Larangan Duduk di Meja Tempat Minuman Keras Diminum

'Umar Ibn Al-Khattaab # berkata, 'Rasulullah 2% melarang dua jenis makan: duduk di meja tempat minuman keras diminum, dan seorang laki-laki yang makan sambil berbaring tengkurap." Imam Ahmad meriwayatkan hal yang sama dengan redaksi ini: "Barangsiapa yang beriman kepada Allah dan Hari Akhir, maka janganlah ia duduk di meja tempat minuman keras diminum..." Meskipun seseorang tidak meminum minuman keras, hanya dengan duduk bersama orang-orang yang meminumnya, ia menunjukkan persetujuan diam-diam dan penerimaannya terhadap perbuatan buruk mereka. Dan itulah hikmah di balik larangan tersebut.

1) Diriwayatkan oleh Abu Daawood (3774), dan Al-Albaanee memberikan penilaian 'sahih' terhadap riwayatnya; dan oleh Ibn Maajah (3370), namun tanpa menyebutkan bagian pertama dari hadith tersebut.

Adab Masuk Kamar Kecil

Salmaan # berkata, "[Salah seorang] dari orang-orang kafir berkata kepada kami, 'Sungguh, kami melihat bahwa sahabat kalian (Nabi #%) bahkan mengajarkan kalian cara buang hajat."" Salmaan # menjawabnya, "Ya, beliau melarang setiap kami dari membersihkan diri [setelah buang air kecil atau buang air besar] dengan tangan kanannya dan dari menghadap Kiblat [saat buang air kecil atau buang air besar]. Dan beliau melarang penggunaan kotoran atau tulang [untuk bersuci]. Dan beliau #¢ bersabda,
content_ar: The people of العلم have mentioned some of the wisdoms behind the prohibition of drinking directly from the mouth of a jug, and we will mention a few of them here. أولاً, the more people drink from and breathe into a jug, canteen, or bottle, the worse will become its smell, حتى it becomes لذا bad that one will feel aversion at the idea of drinking from it. ثانياً, a large ; canteen or jug might have filth, dirt, or insects inside of it, anda person who drinks from it won't be able to notice those things, : and ونتيجة لذلك, his الصحة might become affected. But إذا he pours - the drink into a glass, he will be able to see the filth and remove . "1! Related by Bukhaaree (5627), أحمد (7113), and others. "1 Related by Bukhaaree (5629), أحمد (1990), At-Tirmidhee (1825), An- - Nassaaee (4448), Abu Daawood (3719), Ibn Maajah (3421), and Ad- الباب - 9 The الآداب Of Eating And Drinking 223 it. ثالثاً, a person’s spit might get mixed into the drink, and others will feel disgusted ونتيجة لذلك. رابعاً, the spit and breath of the person drinking might make someone else fall ill, for it is a medically established fact that contagious diseases sometimes spread through saliva and breath. Question: ثبت in an authentic الحديث that النبي ﷺ # drank from a hanging canteen. How then can we find harmony between his action -~ which indicates permissibility - and his verbal prohibition? Answer: Refuting the claim that the action abrogates the verbal prohibition in this issue, Ibn Hajr said, “In Sharhut-Tirmidhee, our Shaikh said, ‘علينا make a distinction between situations wherein one has no excuse - لذا that the prohibition stands - and situations wherein one has an excuse, مثل عندما a canteen is hanging up, and the person who wants to drink cannot easily find a cup and is not positioned such that he can drink from his hand. عندما one has an excuse, it is not disliked for him to drink from the mouth of a canteen, as is the case in the aforementioned Ahadeeth.’ This view [of our Shaikh] is corroborated by the fact that, in all of the Ahadeeth that indicate permissibility, the canteen was hanging up. [علينا keep in العقل that] drinking from a hanging container is more specific (وهكذا allowing it to take a different ruling) than drinking from a container in general. بالتالي, nothing in those Ahadeeth points to a general, comprehensive ruling of permissibility; instead, permissibility applies to that one situation only. [In finding harmony between the apparently conflicting narrations] it is better to construe some of the narrations as dealing with cases of necessity, وهكذا bringing complete harmony between the different narrations, than to take the view of abrogation. And الله 3 knows best.’ " The الحديث is related by At-Tirmidhee (1892) with the fallowing wording: Kabshah Al-Ansaariyyah 4 said, “الرسول ﷺ of الله #% entered upon me and then drank, standing up, from the mouth of a hanging canteen...” Abu ‘Eesa At-Tirmidhee said, “This الحديث is حسن Sahech.” Tbn Maajah related it أيضاً (3423), and Al-Albaanee gave it a ‘صحيح’ grade. 18) The Person Who Is Serving Drinks To Others Should Be The Last Of Them To Drink Himself The basis of this ruling is a long الحديث related by Qataadah #, in which he 4 said, ‘الرسول ﷺ of الله #% began to pour and I to hand them the drink, حتى no one remained save me and save الرسول ﷺ of الله #%. Then الرسول ﷺ of الله # poured [again] and said [to me], ‘Drink,’ to which I answered, ‘I will not drink حتى you drink, O Messenger of الله.’ He # said, Gy Biel pili gale Sp “The person who gives drink to the people is (i.e., should be) the last of them to drink.’ لذا I drank and [then] الرسول ﷺ of الله 3% drank...”") 19) It ls Recommended To Talk خلال A Meal In Thyaa Uloom Ad-Deen, Al-Ghazaalee pointed out that ينبغي لنا talk خلال a meal لذا as to differ from people of foreign nations, for it customary for them to remain silent خلال meals.) And we have been ordered not to imitate non-المسلمون. Tbn Muflaih related that Ishaaq Ibn Ibraaheem said, “I حين had dinner with Abu ‘Abdur-Rahmaan (أحمد ابن حنبل) and a relative of his. عندما we stopped talking, he ate and continued to say, ‘الحمد لله (All praise is to الله)’ and ‘بسم الله (In the Name of الله).’ He then said, ‘Eating and praising [الله] is better than eating and remaining silent.” بعد relating this account, Ibn Muflaih said, “I found nothing related from أحمد that explicitly conflicted with this narration, nor did I find any opposition from most of Al-Ashaab (العلماء that followed الإمام أحمد’s principles of jurisprudence). It appears that أحمد - may الله have الرحمة on him - followed some narration in this '] Related by مسلم (681), أحمد (22040), At-Tirmidhee (1894), Ibn Maajah (3434), and Ad-Daarimee (2135). Some of them related it in its full length, بينما others only related the portion that contains proof for the issue in question. And yet others related it both ways, in its full length and in truncated form. 1 Refer to Thyaa ‘Uloom Ad-Deen (2/11) الباب - 9 The الآداب Of Eating And Drinking 225 regard, for it was his الطريق and wont to search out for ways للاتباع [النبي ﷺ #].”” 20) Eating Together As A Group يستحب for people to eat together as a group, for doing لذا causes their food to be blessed: the more the people are that are sharing a meal, the more the البركات. Jaabir Ibn ‘Abdullah 4 reported that he heard الرسول ﷺ of الله # say, “The food of one is enough for two; the food of two is enough for four; and the food of four is enough for eight." The link between a meal being blessed and people sharing a meal is more explicitly worded in this narration, which At-Tabaraanee relates from the الحديث of Ibn ‘عمر : “Eat together and do not part from بعضهم بعضاً, for indeed, the food of one is sufficient for two...” We learn from this الحديث that it is the البركات of gathering and sharing that lead to a seemingly small amount of food being sufficient for many; ومن ثَم, the more the people there are that are sharing, the more البركات will there be in their meal. Wahshee Ibn Harb related from his الأب, who related from his الأب, that the الصحابة of الرسول ﷺ of الله 2 said, “O Messenger of الله, we eat but do not become filled.” He # said, “Perhaps you separate from بعضهم بعضاً [عندما you eat].” قالوا, “Yes.” He 2% said, 1 Related by مسلم (2059), أحمد (13810), At-Tirmidhee (1820), Ibn Maajah (3254), and Ad-Daarimee (2044). “Then gather upon your food (i.e., eat together) and mention الله’s name over it, and ونتيجة لذلك, it will become blessed for “Al 21) يُكره To Eat A Lot Of Food Or To Eat لذا Little That One's الجسد Weakens Eating أيضاً much leads to many diseases, causes laziness, making one feel it heavy and burdensome to do deeds of obedience to الله 4é, and hardens a person's القلب - and we seek refuge in الله #€ from all of that. The opposite الطريق, eating أيضاً little, أيضاً weakens a person’s الجسد, making him أيضاً weak to do acts of obedience to الله 4%. The best الدواء and prevention is what is prescribed by النبي ﷺ #%, and were we للاتباع النبي ﷺ’s الطريق, we would not, in many instances, have to pay a visit to the doctor. Miqdaam Ibn Ma’dee Karib 4 reported that he heard الرسول ﷺ of الله # say, “A human being has never filled a container that is worse than his stomach, It is sufficient for the son of آدم to have just that amount of morsels that cause his back to remain upright. But إذا there is no escape (i.e., he wants more), then one ثالثاً for his food, one ثالثاً for his drink, and one ثالثاً for his breathing.’! We would do we" to heed what some of our pious predecessors had to say about this subject. Ibn ‘Abdul-Barr and others related that, يوماً ما, عندما ‘عمر Ibn Al-Khattaab # delivered a sermon, قال, ‘‘Beware of gluttony, for it makes one lazy from الصلاة and it is harmful to the الجسد. Upon you is moderation in your food, for that will keep you further away "] Related by Abu Daawood (3764), and Al-Albaanee gave a ‘Sakeeh’ grade to his narration; أحمد (15648); and Ibn Maajah (3286). "1 Related by At-Tirmidhee (2380), who said, “حسن صحيح”; by Tbn Maajah (3349), and Al-Albaanee gave a ‘صحيح’ grade to his narration; and by الباب - 9 The الآداب Of Eating And Drinking 207 from ungratefulness, healthier in your الجسد, and stronger to do worship. And a person does not become destroyed حتى he prefers his desire over his religion.” ‘Alee 2 said, ‘The stomach is the tank of the الجسد: liquids come into it and flow out of it. إذا it is healthy, liquids will flow out of it in a healthy manner, but إذا it is sick, liquids will flow out of it with sickness.” Fudail Ibn ‘Iyaadh said, “Two actions harden the القلب: much talk and much eating.” And it was said to الإمام أحمد, “These are the people who eat very little food.” قال, “That does not impress me. I heard ‘Abdur-Rahmaan Ibn Mahdee say, ‘A group of people did the same and it resulted in them being cut off from obligatory duties (ie., their bodies became لذا weak that they became negligent in performing obligatory duties).” 22) The Prohibition Of Sitting At A Table Upon Which Alcohol Is Imbibed ‘عمر Ibn Al-Khattaab # said, ‘الرسول ﷺ of الله 2% forbade two kinds of eating: sitting at a table upon which alcohol is imbibed, and for a man to eat بينما he is lying prone on his stomach." الإمام أحمد related the same with this wording: “Whosoever believes in الله and the Last Day, then let him not sit at a table upon which alcohol is imbibed...” Even عندما one doesn’t drink alcohol, just by sitting with others who are drinking it, he shows his tacit acceptance and approval of their الشر action. And that is the الحكمة behind the prohibition. 1) Related by Abu Daawood (3774), and Al-Albaanee gave a ‘صحيح’ grade to his narration; and by Ibn Maajah (3370), but without mention of the أولاً part of the الحديث. The الآداب Of Going To The Bathroom Salmaan # said, “[One of] the disbelievers said to us, ‘Verily, we see that your companion (النبي ﷺ #%) even teaches you how to relieve yourselves.”” Salmaan # answered him, “Yes, he forbids each of us from cleaning himself [بعد urinating or defecating] with his الحق hand and from facing the القبلة [خلال the act of urinating or defecating]. And he prohibited the use of manure or bones [for cleaning]. And he #¢ said,
